review.summary: The added qtest coverage is directionally useful, but the new assertion likely reads the QMP error description from the wrong dictionary level. If `qtest_qmp_assert_failure_ref()` returns the full QMP reply, this test will abort on a missing top-level `desc` key instead of validating the intended incompatibility error.

stages.A: Patch intent is clear and appropriate: add a qtest for the existing mapped-ram/postcopy incompatibility in both capability-order permutations.
stages.B: Registration and VM setup look consistent with lightweight source-only validation testing, and reusing a single source VM is reasonable here.
stages.C: The helper structure is generally fine, but the new failure-path inspection appears to assume a different return shape from `qtest_qmp_assert_failure_ref()` than qtest code commonly uses.
stages.D: This is blocking because it can make the new test fail for the wrong reason or become fragile to QMP reply structure rather than the migration validation being tested.
stages.E: No additional functional regressions are evident from the patch alone beyond the reply-parsing concern.

findings.title: QMP failure description is likely read from the wrong dict
findings.description: `qtest_qmp_assert_failure_ref()` commonly returns the full QMP reply object, with the actual error payload nested under the `error` key. The new code reads `desc` directly from `rsp`, which would assert on a missing key instead of checking the expected migration error text. That would make the test fail for the reply layout rather than for the mapped-ram/postcopy validation behavior.
findings.suggestion: Fetch the nested error object before reading the description, for example by using `QDict *err = qdict_get_qdict(rsp, "error");` and then `qdict_get_str(err, "desc")`, unless this helper is explicitly documented to return the nested error dict already.
findings.confidence_reason: The concern depends on the exact contract of `qtest_qmp_assert_failure_ref()`, but in QEMU qtest code the failure reply is typically a top-level QMP object containing an `error` sub-dictionary rather than a flat dict with `desc` at the top level.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
